content.problemStatement:
You are given a rooted tree of NNN vertices, numbered 111 to NNN. The tree is rooted at vertex 111.

For a subset SSS, define f(S)f(S)f(S) as the unique set satisfying the following conditions:


S‚äÜf(S)S \subseteq f(S)S‚äÜf(S)
For all x,y‚ąąf(S)x, y \in f(S)x,y‚ąąf(S), it is true that lca‚Ä†(x,y)‚ąąf(S)\text{lca}^{\dagger}(x, y) \in f(S)lca‚Ä†(x,y)‚ąąf(S).
Among all sets satisfying the above 222 conditions, ‚ą£f(S)‚ą£|f(S)|‚ą£f(S)‚ą£ is as small as possible.
For each K=1,2,‚Ä¶,NK = 1, 2, \ldots, NK=1,2,‚Ä¶,N, find the number of subsets SSS such that ‚ą£f(S)‚ą£=K|f(S)| = K‚ą£f(S)‚ą£=K. Because the answers can be large, print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

‚Ä†^{\dagger}‚Ä† lca(x,y)\text{lca}(x, y)lca(x,y) is defined as the unique node zzz which has the largest value of dep(z)\text{dep}(z)dep(z) among all nodes that are part of both the paths (1,x)(1, x)(1,x) and (1,y)(1, y)(1,y). dep(z)\text{dep}(z)dep(z) is defined as the length of the path (1,z)(1, z)(1,z).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - the number of nodes.
The i‚ąíthi-thi‚ąíth of the next N‚ąí1N - 1N‚ąí1 lines contain 222 integers each - uiu_iui‚Äč and viv_ivi‚Äč, representing an edge in the tree.




Output Format
For each test case, output NNN integers on a new line - the number of subsets for each K=1,2,‚Ä¶,NK = 1, 2, \ldots, NK=1,2,‚Ä¶,N modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The given set of input edges define a tree.
The sum of N2N^2N2 over all test cases does not exceed 250022500^225002.
